title: epilobium roseum var. subsessile
intro: Epilobium roseum var. subsessile is a variety of the Epilobium roseum plant, also known as the bog bean or rose willowherb, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the willowherb family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The health benefits of Epilobium roseum var. subsessile include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve urinary function, and potentially help with symptoms associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts are also used in capsule and tablet forms.
